sheets.name: Complaint Log 2024
rows: Applicant alleges mortgage denial despite strong credit score. Claims branch representative provided no clear explanation.
Borrower reports higher interest rate compared to similarly qualified neighbor in adjacent non-minority tract.
Application denied for property in LMI census tract. Applicant met all documented underwriting criteria.
Applicant claims loan officer steered them toward FHA product despite qualifying for conventional loan.
Home improvement loan denied. Applicant notes similar applications from non-minority areas were approved.
Alleges institution deliberately avoids marketing mortgage products in this census tract.
Rate lock expired during extended processing delay. Replacement rate was 45bps higher.
Third denial for this tract in 60 days. Applicant references pattern of denials in this neighborhood.
General pricing complaint. Tract is outside LAR review population.
Denial complaint from rural tract. Not in the review MSA.
Applicant felt directed toward a specific contractor-affiliated loan product.
Customer alleges systematic pattern of denials targeting minority neighborhoods. References multiple neighbors with similar experiences. Requests regulatory investigation.
Disagrees with appraisal value used in pricing. Tract not in sensitive area.
Standard denial complaint. Tract is outside concentrated review area.
General redlining allegation. Limited supporting detail. Tract not in LAR overlap.

headers.subject: URGENT: Fair Lending Exam Preliminary Findings

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
CONFIDENTIAL - EXAM IN PROGRESS

Team,

The OCC examination team has shared preliminary findings from the
ongoing fair lending examination. Key findings requiring immediate
attention:

1. DENIAL RATE DISPARITY: The examiner identified a denial rate
   disparity of 2.8x for Black applicants compared to non-Hispanic
   White applicants in census tract 55079000100. This exceeds the
   2.0x threshold that triggers enhanced scrutiny under the
   interagency fair lending examination procedures.

2. The examination team recommends an immediate remediation plan
   be developed and presented to the Fair Lending Committee within
   30 days.

3. Additional analysis of pricing disparities in the same tract
   is still in progress.

Please treat this as highest priority. The formal examination
report is expected within 60 days.

- Office of Compliance Risk Management

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
FAIR LENDING COMMITTEE — MEETING MINUTES
Date: March 22, 2024  |  Location: Conference Room 4B

Attendees: [list omitted for brevity]

AGENDA ITEM 3: Pricing Exception Review Thresholds

Motion: To increase the pricing exception review threshold from
$5,000 to $10,000 for census tracts with greater than 30% minority
population concentration. Rationale: Current $5K threshold generates
excessive false positives and diverts examiner resources from
higher-risk exceptions.

Vote: 4-2 in favor. Motion carried.
Implementation: Effective Q2 2024 pending policy update.

Dissenting members noted that raising the threshold during an
active examination period could create adverse optics.

AGENDA ITEM 4: Q1 Lending Pattern Review
[Discussion of seasonal trends — no action items]

AGENDA ITEM 5: Examiner Staffing
[HR update on open positions — no action items]

Minutes prepared by the Risk Committee Secretariat.

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
Jordan,

Per your request, I've completed the updated redlining risk
assessment for our MSA. Three census tracts now meet the
criteria for "redlining risk" classification:

1. 55079000100 — Highest risk. Denial rate 2.8x disparity,
   declining lending volume, multiple consumer complaints.
2. 55079000201 — Moderate risk. Elevated denial rate for
   minority applicants, CRA underperformance.
3. 55025001202 — Watch list. Steering complaints received,
   but denial rates within acceptable range.

I recommend enhanced monitoring for all three tracts,
with monthly reporting to the Fair Lending Committee for
tract 55079000100.

Anna Kowalski
Fair Lending Analytics

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
Jordan,

Forwarding this complaint for your review. This is the escalated
version that was sent to the regional compliance director.

The customer alleges: "systematic pattern of denials targeting
minority neighborhoods." The complaint specifically references
conversations with multiple neighbors in the same census tract
who experienced similar denials over the past 18 months. The
customer is requesting a formal regulatory investigation.

This complaint originated in tract 55079000100 which is already
on our watch list. Given the severity of the allegations, I think
this needs to be included in the committee package as a standalone
item.

Let me know if you need the full complaint file.

James Moretti
Consumer Complaints Unit

headers.subject: Branch CRA Action Plans - Sensitive Tracts

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
TO: Fair Lending Review Team
RE: CRA Performance Action Plans — Branches in Sensitive Tracts

The following branches have been rated "Needs to Improve" in
the most recent CRA examination cycle:

1. WI-BR-002 (Tract 55079000201)
   - Small-business lending at 52% of goal
   - Corrective plan submitted; 2 additional outreach events
     scheduled for Q1 2025

2. WI-BR-003 (Tract 55079000100)
   - Small-business lending at 40% of goal
   - CRITICAL: Failure to meet small-business lending targets
     in LMI tracts for two consecutive examination periods
   - Community outreach significantly below target (2 of 6)
   - Immediate remediation plan required per examiner directive

3. Central Wisconsin Bank branch (Tract 55101000400)
   [Note: This is a peer institution reference included for
   benchmarking — not our branch]

Branches WI-BR-001 and WI-BR-004 both received "Satisfactory"
ratings and require no corrective action at this time.

— Branch Operations & CRA Compliance

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
Q3 2024 Performance Report — Branch WI-BR-003
847 N. 35th St, Milwaukee, WI 53208

Community Engagement Summary:
- Community outreach events held: 2 (target: 6)
- Events cancelled due to staffing: 3
- Event planned but not executed: 1

CRA Small-Business Lending:
- Year-to-date originations: 22 loans
- Annual goal: 55 loans
- Achievement rate: 40% of goal

The branch manager has cited persistent staffing challenges
and a branch renovation project as primary factors. A staffing
plan has been submitted for Q1 2025.

Note: This branch is located in census tract 55079000100, which
is classified as a sensitive tract under our fair lending
monitoring framework.

— Regional Operations Division

headers.subject: RE: Q3 HMDA Data Quality Review

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
Hi Jordan,

Following up on the Q3 data quality review — I found an issue with
the geocoding vendor's output for tract 55079000100.

Specifically, 14 applications in tract 55079000100 had incomplete
geocoding due to a ZIP+4 parsing error in the vendor's March batch.
I've confirmed with the vendor that these records should have been
coded to this tract.

The practical impact: our reported denial count for this tract may
be higher by 3 additional applications (which were coded to adjacent
tract 55079000201 incorrectly). The corrected denial count should be
reflected in any fair lending analysis.

I've attached the reconciliation workbook but wanted to flag this
via email as well since it affects the Q4 committee package.

Best,
Sarah Nguyen
Data Quality & Reporting

headers.subject: Privileged: Fair Lending Risk Exposure Estimate

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL

Dear Jordan,

As requested, our firm has completed a preliminary assessment of
the institution's potential regulatory exposure arising from the
fair lending findings in the Milwaukee-Waukesha MSA.

Based on the current complaint volume (15 complaints, 8 in LAR
tracts), the denial rate disparity patterns, and recent DOJ
enforcement precedents in similar cases, we estimate the
potential regulatory fine exposure at $2.5M to $4.0M.

This estimate assumes:
- No proactive self-reporting to regulators
- Current complaint trajectory continues
- Examination confirms preliminary disparity findings

If the institution elects proactive self-reporting and
demonstrates meaningful remediation efforts, the estimated
exposure could be reduced by 40-60%.

We strongly recommend discussing this with the Board Risk
Committee at the earliest opportunity.

Regards,
Katherine Wells, Partner
Financial Services Regulatory Practice

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
Fair Lending Review Team,

We've completed the spatial lending analysis for tract
55079000100 as requested. Key findings:

LENDING VOLUME TREND:
- 2023 originations in tract: 31 loans
- 2024 originations in tract: 20 loans
- Year-over-year change: -35% decline in lending volume

APPLICATION VOLUME TREND:
- 2023 applications: 38
- 2024 applications: 43 (estimated based on partial Q4)
- Year-over-year change: +12% increase in applications

This presents an INVERSE CORRELATION: applications are
increasing while originations are declining, which is
consistent with an elevated denial rate pattern.

The heatmap visualization is available on the analytics
dashboard (internal portal, restricted access). We can
present this at the committee meeting if needed.

— Data Analytics & Business Intelligence

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.text:
INTERNAL AUDIT FINDING — PRICING EXCEPTIONS

The December 2024 internal audit of pricing exceptions has been
completed. Summary of findings:

Census Tract 55079000100:
- Total pricing exceptions reviewed: 9
- Exceptions with adequate documentation: 2
- Exceptions lacking adequate justification: 7

The 7 insufficiently documented exceptions represent a
documentation deficiency rate of 78% for this tract, compared
to the institution-wide average of 23%.

Recommended action: All 7 exceptions should be flagged in the
fair lending committee package with a request for post-hoc
justification from the originating loan officers.

This finding has been reported to the Chief Compliance Officer.

— Internal Audit Division

Fair-Lending Testing Methodology

Q4 HMDA Exception Package
First National Bank — Compliance Risk Management
Effective Date: December 2024

Apply these definitions exactly when rebuilding the committee package from the records. Where the prior committee summary conflicts with what these rules produce from the extract, the extract governs — show the corrected result and mark the prior assertion accordingly.

Population & Decisioning

  • Transaction population = every row in hmda_lar_extract_2024.csv.
  • Action-taken codes (see hmda_action_code_reference.docx): 1 = originated, 2 = approved-not-accepted, 3 = denied, 4 = withdrawn, 5 = closed-incomplete, 6 = purchased loan, 8 = preapproval-denied.
  • Decision denominator = all applications excluding purchased loans (code 6).
  • Denial rate = denied applications (code 3) ÷ decision denominator, shown as a percentage rounded to one decimal place.

Tract Context

Join to ffiec_census_tract_extract_2025.csv on Census_Tract:

  • LMI tract = census LMI_Flag == "Y".
  • Majority-minority tract (MMCT) = census MMCT_Flag == "Y".
  • Sensitive tract = LMI_Flag == "Y" OR MMCT_Flag == "Y".
  • If a HMDA tract does not join to the census file, treat it as a data-quality issue (do not impute).

Exception Definitions (Further Review)

  • Pricing exception = an originated loan (code 1) with Rate_Spread ≥ 1.5. (This is the higher-priced-mortgage-loan threshold.)
  • Denial exception = a denied application (code 3) located in a sensitive tract (LMI or majority-minority).
  • Further-review record = any record that is a pricing exception or a denial exception. List one row per further-review record in Exception_Log with its Record_ID and Exception_Type (Pricing exception / Denial exception).

Tract Concentration

  • For each tract, count its further-review records.
  • Concentration follow-up list = the 4 tracts with the most further-review records. Identify the single highest tract as the Highest-review tract.

Prior-Summary Reconciliation

For each material assertion in prior_committee_summary.docx, mark it Supported or Contradicted by the extract. In particular, validate the prior claim that there was no material fair-lending exception pattern.

Committee Recommendation

If the corrected analysis shows a concentrated, record-level pattern of pricing and/or denial exceptions in sensitive tracts, the recommendation is: "Escalate to fair-lending committee with record-level follow-up".
messages: Message(type='warning', message='Unrecognised paragraph style: Title (Style ID: Title)')

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.html:
HMDA Action Code and Review Reference

This reference is used for the committee workbook. It defines the coding and review thresholds for the supplied extract.

Action-taken code handling

Code

Action label

Committee handling

1

Loan originated

Include in pricing review.

2

Application approved but not accepted

Count separately from denials.

3

Application denied

Include in denial-rate and denial-exception review.

4

Application withdrawn by applicant

Exclude from denial-rate numerator.

5

File closed for incompleteness

Exclude from denial-rate numerator.

6

Purchased loan

Exclude from application decision testing.

7

Preapproval request denied

Use only if present in the source file.

8

Preapproval request approved but not accepted

Use only if present in the source file.

Review thresholds

Rule

Treatment

Pricing exception threshold

Rate spread greater than or equal to 1.50 percentage points for originated loans.

Severe pricing threshold

Rate spread greater than or equal to 2.00 percentage points for originated loans.

Majority-minority tract flag

Tract minority population percentage greater than or equal to 50%.

LMI tract flag

Tract income level of Low or Moderate.

Concentration screen

A tract or segment requires follow-up when it has sufficient volume and its denial rate meets or exceeds the testing threshold.

Further review treatment

Record-level follow-up is required when a denial or pricing exception occurs in a protected or sensitive tract context.

Thresholds are testing triggers. They do not establish unlawful discrimination. The committee workbook should describe required follow-up and evidence gaps, not make legal conclusions.

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.html:
HMDA testing notes — fair lending packet

Prepared by Compliance Testing / retail lending review queue

Current use

Working notes for the analyst assigned to rebuild the committee file; not a findings summary.

Source controls

Use the HMDA extract and census tract file as the control files. Prior narrative is background only.

Known caveat

Some earlier statements were written before tract attributes and action-code treatment were locked down.

Review posture

The committee packet should be rebuilt from the actual records, not refreshed from the prior write-up. Treat the prior committee note as an early draft. It may still be useful for context, but I would not rely on its counts or segment statements without tying them back to the extract.

Main concern from the last working session: the data cut has tract fields now, and the old summary did not really deal with tract context. The review should distinguish plain operational noise from records that deserve a second look because the action outcome, pricing, and tract indicators line up in a way the committee will ask about.

Analyst reminders

Use the HMDA file as the transaction population. Use the census file for tract income and tract minority concentration. If a tract value is missing or does not join cleanly, flag it as a data-quality issue rather than filling it in from memory.

Do not bring in external web research or policy commentary. The committee asked for a contained testing file. Keep the focus on whether the supplied extract supports the prior summary and which records need follow-up.

A few rows are likely to look benign at first because the action code alone is not enough. Look at rate spread, action taken, borrower proxy fields available in the modified extract, and tract sensitivity together. The file should show the trail clearly enough that another examiner can re-check it from the rows.

Questions to resolve before circulation

Does the prior summary overstate or understate the review population once purchased loans and non-decision outcomes are handled consistently?

Are pricing outliers being treated only as origination QC items, or do any of them sit in contexts that require fair-lending follow-up?

Are denial outcomes concentrated in sensitive tract contexts? If so, identify the records and explain the review reason without making a legal conclusion.

What exactly should Compliance do next for records that remain in scope: request underwriting notes, review pricing discretion, confirm application chronology, or document no further action?

File handling notes

Keep formulas live in the workbook. The committee has been asking for traceable schedules rather than static numbers pasted into a dashboard.

Avoid hiding the disagreement with the prior summary. If a prior assertion is not supported by the final extract, show the corrected result and the recommended follow-up. That reconciliation is the part reviewers will look for first.

No need for a formal memo tone in the working tabs. Short labels, source references, and record IDs are more useful than broad narrative.

Margin note: do not turn these notes into the answer key. They are only here to keep the scope bounded.

hmda_fair_lending_review/required_files/.html:
Prior Committee Summary: HMDA Testing Draft

Status: Working draft for committee follow-up. This draft has not been updated for the final data package.

Prior position

The earlier committee draft treated the portfolio as having no material fair-lending exception pattern. That conclusion should be retested because the final extract now includes tract attributes and updated action-code treatment.

The draft also treated pricing as an origination-quality control issue rather than a fair-lending review item. That assumption may be too narrow if elevated spreads appear in protected or sensitive tract contexts.

Known limitations

  • The draft did not tie action-taken counts to a source-data worksheet.
  • The draft did not reconcile census tract attributes to the loan-application extract.
  • The draft did not include a record-level exception log.
  • The draft did not separate pricing outliers from ordinary originations.

Committee request

Before the next meeting, Compliance needs a workbook that can be checked cell by cell against the source extracts. The final recommendation should identify the review population, the reason each exception is present, and the next action required.
